message:
test(connectors/copilot-cli): regression coverage for cass#187 Chronicle-format nested `data` payloads

Integration-style tests for the Copilot CLI connector via CASS's re-export of
`franken-agent-detection`, locking down the fix landed upstream in commit
88756ba9 (pinned in the preceding dep-bump commit).

Context (cass#187): Copilot CLI's Chronicle session-state format stores
events under `~/.copilot/session-state/<uuid>/events.jsonl`, and each event
nests the message payload under a `data` object rather than flat at the
event root:

    {"event":"message","timestamp":"…","data":{"role":"user","content":"…"}}

Prior to the fix, the connector looked for `content` at the event root,
which matched the older flat format. Chronicle events yielded zero
conversations — sessions from users on the current Copilot CLI release
were invisible to `cass search` until this test / fix series.

Cases in the new file:

  * `scan_parses_chronicle_nested_data_content` — canonical reproduction
    of the cass#187 shape. Writes a session dir with a Chronicle
    events.jsonl (ISO8601 timestamps, data.role/data.content nesting) and
    asserts the connector surfaces the user and assistant turns with the
    right attribution and timestamp parsing.

  * (Adjacent cases in the same file locks down the existing flat-format
    parsing path and the mixed-format session case, so the Chronicle
    branch cannot regress the prior behavior.)

No production code changes in this commit — the upstream fix is already in
the pinned rev of `franken-agent-detection`. This test is the regression
guard that would turn the bug back into a CI failure if the pin were ever
reverted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
